mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter through higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or forward 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity facing higher financing costs, forcing equity valuations lower over the next month as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Negative forward 20-day returns fail to materialize during tightening cycles when INDPRO declines, with mean returns exceeding +0.5 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger immediate market repricing of future central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings expansion.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or mean returns are negative during payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining retail sales signals a synchronized demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined weak demand signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in more than 60 percent of triggered cases over a 4-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices surpassing 95.0 indicate robust manufacturing activity and supply chain efficiency, prompting immediate positive equity repricing over the next five trading days as growth momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
